input.description: Run shared workspace unit test suite
Show status and diff for shared changes
Show status then diff for shared files

content.text:
Implemented `T1` in `@memories/shared` with concrete, exported Zod contracts and edge-case tests.

### What I changed

- Expanded `packages/shared/src/index.ts` to add shared API contracts for:
  - **Cursor pagination**: `cursorSchema`, `cursorPaginationQuerySchema`, and related constants.
  - **Error body**: `apiErrorSchema` aligned to `{ code, message, request_id }`.
  - **Memory/media DTOs** for list/detail flows:
    - `memoryMediaDescriptorSchema`
    - `memorySummarySchema`
    - `memoryListResponseSchema`
    - `memoryDetailSchema`
    - `memoryDetailResponseSchema`
    - `memoryTranscriptSchema` and `transcriptStatusSchema`
- Exported all associated inferred types (`MemoryListResponse`, `MemoryDetailResponse`, `ApiError`, etc.) and validation constants (max lengths, page sizes).

### Tests added

Updated `packages/shared/src/index.test.ts` with new coverage for T1 edge cases:

- empty list response parses
- cursor over max length is rejected
- default pagination size is applied
- title at max length is accepted
- title above max length is rejected
- max-length error message is accepted

### Verification run

- `npm run test -w @memories/shared` ✅ (8/8 passing)
- `npm run typecheck -w @memories/shared` ✅
- Lints for touched files: no issues

If you want, I can proceed directly to `T2` next.

<timestamp>Thursday, Apr 30, 2026, 11:41 AM (UTC-7)</timestamp>
<user_query>
Can you implement Prompt T2
</user_query>

I’ll implement `T2` end-to-end next: first I’ll read the backend/database skill guidance, then inspect current API data-layer setup and add Drizzle schema + migrations for `memories`, `memory_media`, `audit_events`, plus transcript/job stubs.

I’ve confirmed there’s no existing DB layer yet, so I’m now adding Drizzle to `apps/api`, creating the schema/tables and migration scaffolding, then I’ll run generate/typecheck and attempt migrate verification.

@@
   "scripts": {
     "dev": "cross-env LOG_LEVEL=warn tsx watch src/index.ts",
     "build": "tsc",
     "start": "node dist/index.js",
+    "db:generate": "drizzle-kit generate --config=drizzle.config.ts",
+    "db:migrate": "drizzle-kit migrate --config=drizzle.config.ts",
+    "db:studio": "drizzle-kit studio --config=drizzle.config.ts",
     "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit",
     "test": "vitest run"
   },
*** End Patch

content.name: ApplyPatch
content.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: C:/Users/Ken Levy/OneDrive/Documents/Business/Ohana/Memories/apps/api/src/db/schema.ts
+import { sql } from "drizzle-orm";
+import {
+  index,
+  integer,
+  jsonb,
+  pgEnum,
+  pgTable,
+  text,
+  timestamp,
+  uuid,
+  varchar,
+} from "drizzle-orm/pg-core";
+
+export const memoryMediaTypeEnum = pgEnum("memory_media_type", [
+  "image",
+  "audio",
+  "video",
+]);
+
+export const transcriptStatusEnum = pgEnum("transcript_status", [
+  "pending",
+  "ready",
+  "failed",
+]);
+
+export const transcriptionJobStatusEnum = pgEnum("transcription_job_status", [
+  "pending",
+  "processing",
+  "completed",
+  "failed",
+]);
+
+export const memories = pgTable(
+  "memories",
+  {
+    id: uuid("id").primaryKey(),
+    practiceId: uuid("practice_id").notNull(),
+    clientId: uuid("client_id").notNull(),
+    title: varchar("title", { length: 120 }).notNull(),
+    room: varchar("room", { length: 80 }),
+    body: text("body"),
+    sharingVisibility: varchar("sharing_visibility", { length: 32 })
+      .notNull()
+      .default("practice"),
+    createdAt: timestamp("created_at", { withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
+    updatedAt: timestamp("updated_at", { withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
+    deletedAt: timestamp("deleted_at", { withTimezone: true }),
+  },
+  (table) => ({
+    practiceClientCreatedIdx: index("memories_practice_client_created_idx").on(
+      table.practiceId,
+      table.clientId,
+      table.createdAt,
+    ),
+    clientCreatedIdx: index("memories_client_created_idx").on(table.clientId, table.createdAt),
+  }),
+);
+
+export const memoryMedia = pgTable(
+  "memory_media",
+  {
+    id: uuid("id").primaryKey(),
+    memoryId: uuid("memory_id")
+      .notNull()
+      .references(() => memories.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
+    practiceId: uuid("practice_id").notNull(),
+    clientId: uuid("client_id").notNull(),
+    type: memoryMediaTypeEnum("type").notNull(),
+    storageKey: varchar("storage_key", { length: 512 }).notNull(),
+    mimeType: varchar("mime_type", { length: 128 }).notNull(),
+    byteSize: integer("byte_size").notNull(),
+    sortOrder: integer("sort_order").notNull().default(0),
+    createdAt: timestamp("created_at", { withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
+    deletedAt: timestamp("deleted_at", { withTimezone: true }),
+  },
+  (table) => ({
+    memorySortOrderIdx: index("memory_media_memory_sort_order_idx").on(table.memoryId, table.sortOrder),
+    practiceClientIdx: index("memory_media_practice_client_idx").on(table.practiceId, table.clientId),
+  }),
+);
+
+export const memoryTranscripts = pgTable(
+  "memory_transcripts",
+  {
+    memoryId: uuid("memory_id")
+      .primaryKey()
+      .references(() => memories.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
+    practiceId: uuid("practice_id").notNull(),
+    clientId: uuid("client_id").notNull(),
+    status: transcriptStatusEnum("status").notNull().default("pending"),
+    text: text("text"),
+    confidence: integer("confidence"),
+    vendorReference: varchar("vendor_reference", { length: 255 }),
+    createdAt: timestamp("created_at", { withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
+    updatedAt: timestamp("updated_at", { withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
+  },
+  (table) => ({
+    practiceClientStatusIdx: index("memory_transcripts_practice_client_status_idx").on(
+      table.practiceId,
+      table.clientId,
+      table.status,
+    ),
+  }),
+);
+
+export const transcriptionJobs = pgTable(
+  "transcription_jobs",
+  {
+    id: uuid("id").primaryKey(),
+    memoryId: uuid("memory_id")
+      .notNull()
+      .references(() => memories.id, { onDelete: "cascade" }),
+    mediaId: uuid("media_id").references(() => memoryMedia.id, { onDelete: "set null" }),
+    practiceId: uuid("practice_id").notNull(),
+    clientId: uuid("client_id").notNull(),
+    status: transcriptionJobStatusEnum("status").notNull().default("pending"),
+    attemptCount: integer("attempt_count").notNull().default(0),
+    lastError: text("last_error"),
+    createdAt: timestamp("created_at", { withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
+    updatedAt: timestamp("updated_at", { withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
+    startedAt: timestamp("started_at", { withTimezone: true }),
+    completedAt: timestamp("completed_at", { withTimezone: true }),
+  },
+  (table) => ({
+    memoryStatusIdx: index("transcription_jobs_memory_status_idx").on(table.memoryId, table.status),
+    practiceClientCreatedIdx: index("transcription_jobs_practice_client_created_idx").on(
+      table.practiceId,
+      table.clientId,
+      table.createdAt,
+    ),
+  }),
+);
+
+export const auditEvents = pgTable(
+  "audit_events",
+  {
+    id: uuid("id").primaryKey(),
+    practiceId: uuid("practice_id").notNull(),
+    clientId: uuid("client_id").notNull(),
+    memoryId: uuid("memory_id").references(() => memories.id, { onDelete: "set null" }),
+    actorUserId: uuid("actor_user_id"),
+    entityType: varchar("entity_type", { length: 64 }).notNull(),
+    entityId: uuid("entity_id"),
+    action: varchar("action", { length: 64 }).notNull(),
+    requestId: varchar("request_id", { length: 128 }).notNull(),
+    ipAddress: varchar("ip_address", { length: 64 }),
+    metadata: jsonb("metadata").notNull().default(sql`'{}'::jsonb`),
+    createdAt: timestamp("created_at", { withTimezone: true }).notNull().defaultNow(),
+  },
+  (table) => ({
+    practiceClientCreatedIdx: index("audit_events_practice_client_created_idx").on(
+      table.practiceId,
+      table.clientId,
+      table.createdAt,
+    ),
+    requestIdIdx: index("audit_events_request_id_idx").on(table.requestId),
+    memoryCreatedIdx: index("audit_events_memory_created_idx").on(table.memoryId, table.createdAt),
+  }),
+);
*** End Patch

content.name: ApplyPatch

input.command: git status --short; git diff -- apps/api/package.json apps/api/drizzle.config.ts apps/api/src/db/schema.ts apps/api/drizzle/0000_secret_nicolaos.sql apps/api/drizzle/meta/_journal.json .env.example
input.description: Inspect working tree and T2-related diff
Check if psql CLI is installed locally

content.text:
Implemented `T2` in `apps/api` with Drizzle schema + migration scaffolding for the memory domain and transcript/job stubs.

- Added DB tooling to `apps/api/package.json`:
  - `db:generate`, `db:migrate`, `db:studio`
  - dependencies: `drizzle-orm`, `pg`, `drizzle-kit`
- Added Drizzle config at `apps/api/drizzle.config.ts` (reads `DATABASE_URL`).
- Added schema at `apps/api/src/db/schema.ts` with:
  - `memories` (includes `practice_id`, `client_id`, and soft-delete `deleted_at`)
  - `memory_media` (enum type supports `image | audio | video`)
  - `audit_events`
  - stubs: `memory_transcripts`, `transcription_jobs`
  - foreign keys and indexes for common access patterns
- Generated migration artifacts in `apps/api/drizzle/`:
  - `0000_secret_nicolaos.sql`
  - `meta/_journal.json`
  - `meta/0000_snapshot.json`
- Updated `.env.example` to include active `DATABASE_URL` / `TEST_DATABASE_URL` entries now that persistence exists.

Validation run:

- `npm run db:generate -w @memories/api` ✅
- `npm run test -w @memories/api` ✅
- `npm run typecheck --workspaces --if-present` ✅
- `npm run db:migrate -w @memories/api` ❌ (expected locally right now: missing `DATABASE_URL` / no local Postgres)

To complete the “apply clean on empty DB” verification locally, set `DATABASE_URL` to a fresh Postgres instance and run:

- `npm run db:migrate -w @memories/api`

<timestamp>Thursday, Apr 30, 2026, 11:47 AM (UTC-7)</timestamp>
<user_query>
Can you setup the DATABASE_URL and run the migration and make that standard practice?
</user_query>
Understood — I’ll try wiring a real local `DATABASE_URL`, run `db:migrate`, and then codify a repeatable “standard practice” in scripts/docs so this is the default setup path going forward.
